_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national autopilot
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize federal batch: ID-01 Fulcher, ID-02 Simpson, IL-01 Jackson, IL-03 Ramirez, IL-04 Garcia (retiring lame duck). All TRUE/FALSE cite roll calls (House Clerk XML), cosponsorships (congress.gov/GovTrack), issue-group ratings/endorsements, FEC/ProPublica itemizer, or the member's own statements. Nulls = no evidence found.

russ-fulcher.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-10. Hard-right record: Life at Conception Act cosponsor (116th-119th), RFMA Nay x2, Equality Act Nay, SAVE Act Aye x2, HR 2 Yea, BSCA/AWB Nay, pistol-brace CRA Yea, CBDC Act Aye, FRA No (fiscal-hawk side), Balanced Budget Amendment cosponsor. Heritage 92% lifetime, SBA A+, GOA A. Deviations: voted NAY on 2002 AUMF repeal despite stated war-powers doctrine; accepted AIPAC PAC money ($11k in 2024 cycle per FEC/ProPublica). On the 2026-05-19 GOP primary ballot vs two challengers (pre-primary cite; result not verified this pass).

0.note: Own issue page: 'Life begins at conception, and every unborn child has the inherent right to life'; Life at Conception Act cosponsor in 116th-119th Congresses.

1.note: Life at Conception Act cosponsorship is personhood/abolition-grade advocacy; Born-Alive Act Yea (roll 29, 2023); WHPA Nay (roll 295, 2021); Defund Planned Parenthood Act cosponsor.

2.note: Repeatedly cosponsored personhood from 'the moment of fertilization' with no IVF carve-out; Idaho record defines destruction from fertilization as abortion. No direct stem-cell vote.

3.note: Sponsored Idaho bill to ban physician-assisted suicide as state senator; official page: pro-life 'at the end of life'.

4.note: FEC itemizer scan of his committee 2020-2026: no Planned Parenthood/NARAL/EMILY's List receipts; SBA Pro-Life America A+.

0.note: Opposed RFMA as forcing Idaho 'to recognize an evolving definition of marriage'; evangelical Christian defending the traditional definition.

1.note: Voted Nay on Respect for Marriage Act both roll calls (373 and 513, 2022).

2.note: Equality Act Nay (2021); Protection of Women and Girls in Sports Yea (2023, 2025); Protect Children's Innocence Act Yea (2025).

3.note: No evidence found on no-fault divorce reform.

4.note: Equality Act Nay; signed letter to block federal funding of transgender-related programs in FY27 spending.

0.note: Cosponsored H.R.899 to terminate the Department of Education (118th and 119th); on record supporting movement toward school choice.

1.note: Parents Bill of Rights Act Aye (2023); voted to criminalize gender-transition procedures on minors (H.R.3492, 2025).

2.note: Signed Education & Labor letter to Sec. Cardona calling CRT 'a racist and divisive ideology' and objecting to CRT-based curriculum priorities.

3.note: TAKE IT DOWN Act Yea (near-unanimous, 2025) noted; no porn age-verification record found - not scored on a routine vote.

4.note: No evidence found on faith-based adoption/foster protections.

0.note: Publicly self-identifies as an evangelical Christian on his official site and frames policy positions from his faith.

1.note: Freedom for Health Care Workers Act Aye (repealed CMS vaccine mandate incl. religious objectors); Nay on Equality Act, which lacked religious-liberty exemptions.

2.note: Protecting Speech from Government Interference Act Yea is censorship-adjacent; no direct compelled-speech (pronoun-mandate) record found.

3.note: No federal record found on public prayer/symbols.

4.note: No evidence found.

0.note: Voted Aye on CBDC Anti-Surveillance State Act (roll 230, 5/23/2024).

1.note: Checked: not a cosponsor of Audit the Fed (116th-118th); no sound-money record found.

2.note: Voted No on Fiscal Responsibility Act debt-limit deal (1 of 71 R, fiscal-hawk side); cosponsored Balanced Budget Amendment (2021); voted against Dec 2024 CR.

3.note: No evidence found on usury limits.

4.note: Yea on CRA disapproving DOL ESG-investing rule (2023); 1 of 39 R voting Yea on Merger Filing Fee Modernization antitrust package (2022).

0.note: No evidence found on hand-counted paper ballots.

1.note: Voted Aye on SAVE Act (proof-of-citizenship voter registration) twice (2024 and 2025).

2.note: No evidence found on single-day in-person voting.

3.note: Voted Nay on H.R.1 For the People Act (national mail-in/drop-box expansion, 2021).

4.note: No private-election-funding record found (not an ACE Act cosponsor).

0.note: H.R.2 Secure the Border Act Yea (wall construction restart); official page: 'I particularly support the 400 new miles of border wall constructed'.

1.note: Laken Riley Act Yea (mandatory detention of criminal aliens) documented, but no position found on deportation of all illegal aliens incl. those who entered as minors.

2.note: Official site: supports 'initiatives to end federal funding to sanctuary cities'.

3.note: H.R.2 Yea; the bill contains mandatory nationwide E-Verify.

4.note: No Fulcher-specific record found on birthright citizenship or foreign farmland ownership.

0.note: Gun Owners of America rating A (no-compromise group whose agenda centers permitless carry); cosponsor of Constitutional Concealed Carry Reciprocity Act (118th, 119th).

1.note: Nay on Bipartisan Safer Communities Act (red-flag funding, 2022); Nay on Assault Weapons Ban (2022).

2.note: Hearing Protection Act cosponsor (removes suppressors from the NFA) - partial NFA rollback; no full NFA/GCA repeal sponsorship found.

3.note: Castle doctrine is a state matter; no federal record found.

4.note: Yea on CRA disapproving ATF pistol-brace rule (2023); introduced the GOA-backed ATF Transparency Act.

1.note: Voted Nay on repealing the 2002 Iraq AUMF (H.R.256, 2021) while 49 Republicans voted yes - contrary to AUMF-repeal position.

3.note: FEC records: AIPAC PAC contributions of $5,000 (2022, $2,000 refunded) and $11,000 across the 2024 cycle.

michael-simpson.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-10. Institutionalist appropriator R with major cross-party deviations: voted YES on Respect for Marriage Act on BOTH roll calls (7/2022 and 12/2022, only ID delegation member); sponsored Fairness for All Act (SOGI in civil-rights law with religious exemptions); 1 of 9 R against D.C. voucher reauthorization (serial teachers-union endorsee); pro-DACA-citizenship quotes; Ukraine aid Yea; Heritage 56% lifetime. Conservative anchors: Life at Conception Act cosponsor, voted AGAINST embryonic stem-cell expansion (2005, 2007), WHPA Nay, Equality Act Nay, HR 2 Yea, SAVE Act Aye, BSCA/AWB Nay, CBDC Act Aye. Won the 2026-05-19 GOP primary with 63.3%.

0.note: Cosponsored the Life at Conception Act (H.R.431, 118th; personhood from conception), announced in his own press release.

1.note: Born-Alive Act Yea (roll 29, 2023); WHPA Nay (roll 295, 2021); Life at Conception cosponsor; NRLC 100%.

2.note: Voted against embryonic stem-cell research funding expansion both times: Nay on H.R.810 (2005) and No on H.R.3 (2007) - verified from Clerk XML.

3.note: Voted Aye on Pain Relief Promotion Act (1999), the bill targeting Oregon-style assisted suicide via controlled-substances law.

4.note: No abortion-PAC receipts found and he cosponsored the Title X Abortion Provider Prohibition Act, but FEC/OpenSecrets data was inaccessible this pass - not asserting 'never' without the scan.

0.note: No one-man-one-woman affirmation found; his public statements defend his RFMA vote on states'-rights/religious-freedom grounds.

1.note: Voted Yea on the Respect for Marriage Act on BOTH roll calls (7/19/2022 and 12/8/2022) - one of 39-47 R crossovers, only Idaho delegation member to do so.

2.note: Equality Act Nay (2021); Protection of Women and Girls in Sports Yea (2023). Caveat: sponsored Fairness for All (SOGI nondiscrimination with religious exemptions).

3.note: No evidence found on no-fault divorce reform.

4.note: Sponsored the Fairness for All Act, codifying sexual orientation/gender identity in federal civil-rights law (with religious carve-outs); 1 of 29 R for trans-inclusive VAWA 2021; RFMA Yea x2.

jonathan-jackson.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-10. Progressive D (Heritage 0% in 119th, RFFA 100%, Giffords-endorsed) with documented deviations: publicly professing Christian (2025 National Prayer Breakfast speaker, son of Rev. Jesse Jackson); said Chicagoans should get a vote on sanctuary status ('let's rescind it' if they choose) and criticized migrant spending; PRIME Act (Massie small-farm bill) cosponsor; Kids Online Safety Act cosponsor; Big Ag antitrust critic; Nay on $26B Israel supplemental while Yea on Ukraine/Indo-Pacific; voted to denounce socialism (H.Con.Res.9). Accepted $15K from AIPAC (2022+2024) and says he'd still accept it. Unopposed in the 3/17/2026 Dem primary; faces R Christian Maxwell 11/3/2026.

delia-ramirez.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-10. Progressive D (Heritage 26% in 118th, RFFA 100, EMILY's List candidate, Giffords/Brady-endorsed) with documented deviations: Yea on the Gaetz Syria war-powers resolution; Iraq-AUMF-repeal cosponsor; Nay on both NDAAs + People Over Pentagon cosponsor; publicly denounces AIPAC money (top contributors are unions; no AIPAC receipts found). First United Methodist Latina in Congress with explicit public professions of Christian faith. Married to a DACA recipient; leading anti-deportation and sanctuary-defense voice; Born in the USA Act sponsor (birthright citizenship). Advanced from the 3/17/2026 Dem primary; on the 11/3/2026 general ballot in IL-03.

0.note: No conception statement; own platform: 'uncompromising supporter of reproductive rights... fights relentlessly to protect abortion access'; Born-Alive Act Nay (2023).

2.note: Cosponsor of the Access to Family Building Act establishing a statutory right to IVF (incl. embryo creation/discard).

3.note: No evidence found on euthanasia/assisted suicide.

4.note: EMILY's List candidate (endorsement/backing by the pro-choice PAC); top contributors otherwise unions per OpenSecrets.

0.note: No marriage-definition statement found (platform supports LGBTQ+ rights generally; scored at q4).

jesus-garcia.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-10. RETIRING: confirmed 2025-11-05 he will not seek reelection in 2026 (cardiologist's advice; wife's MS); timed the announcement after the filing deadline to clear the ballot for his chief of staff, drawing a 238-186 House reprimand on 2025-11-18. Progressive D record (WHPA Yea x2, RFMA Yea x2, Equality Act Yea, HR 2/SAVE/Laken Riley Nay, AWB Yea) with documented aligned positions: Yemen and Iran war-powers resolutions Yea, 2002 AUMF repeal Yea, lead sponsor of the bipartisan 36% national usury cap (with R Glenn Grothman), Bank Merger Review Modernization Act vs too-big-to-fail consolidation, Pocan 10% Pentagon-cut Yea + four NDAA No votes. Authored Cook County's pioneering 2011 sanctuary ordinance.
